questions.question: If matrix $A$ is $2 \times 3$ and matrix $B$ is $3 \times 4$, what are the dimensions of the product $AB$?
questions.explanation: The inner dimensions ($3$) match, so the product is defined. The outer dimensions ($2$ and $4$) determine the result size: $2 \times 4$.

questions.question: Calculate the first element (Row 1, Column 1) of $AB$ if $A = \begin{bmatrix} 1 & 2 \\ 3 & 4 \end{bmatrix}$ and $B = \begin{bmatrix} 5 & 6 \\ 7 & 8 \end{bmatrix}$.
Verify $AI = A$ for $A = \begin{bmatrix} 5 & 2 \\ -1 & 3 \end{bmatrix}$ and $I = \begin{bmatrix} 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 \end{bmatrix}$.
questions.explanation: Multiplying by the identity matrix leaves $A$ unchanged: $AI = A$.

questions.question: For $A = \begin{bmatrix} 1 & 2 & 3 \\ 4 & 5 & 6 \end{bmatrix}$ and $B = \begin{bmatrix} 1 & 0 \\ 2 & 1 \\ 0 & 2 \end{bmatrix}$, find the entry in row 2, column 2 of $AB$.
Calculate $\begin{bmatrix} 0 & 1 \\ 1 & 0 \end{bmatrix} \begin{bmatrix} x \\ y \end{bmatrix}$.
questions.explanation: Row 1: $0(x) + 1(y) = y$. Row 2: $1(x) + 0(y) = x$. This matrix swaps the variables.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have mastered matrix multiplication — determining dimensions, computing full products, finding specific entries, multiplying rectangular matrices, and verifying the identity property.
scoreMessages.70: Good work. Key reminders: (1) Inner dimensions must match: $(a \times b)(b \times c) \to a \times c$, (2) Entry $(i,j)$ = dot product of row $i$ of first matrix with column $j$ of second, (3) Matrix multiplication is not commutative, (4) Multiplying by $I$ leaves the matrix unchanged.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing. Focus on: (1) Checking dimensions before attempting multiplication, (2) Computing the dot product carefully — multiply corresponding entries and sum, (3) Working systematically row by row, column by column, (4) The product of $1 \times n$ and $n \times 1$ is a $1 \times 1$ scalar.
scoreMessages.0: Review matrix multiplication: (1) Dimensions: $(a \times b) \times (b \times c)$ yields $a \times c$ — inner dimensions must match, (2) Entry $(i,j)$ = row $i$ of first matrix dot column $j$ of second, (3) Compute each entry one at a time: multiply pairs and add, (4) Identity matrix $I$: $AI = IA = A$.
